title: The Last One
overview: An orb is tossed upon the earth. It transforms to match its surrounding environment: first a rock, then moss, then a dying wolf. The wolf returns to its last remaining family, a boy living alone in a small home in the snow.

title: A Rambunctious Girl
overview: The orb, having morphed into a boy, finds its way to Ninanna, where he is attacked. He is discovered by a girl, March, who may not achieve her goal of becoming a grown-up.

title: A Small Evolution
overview: March is escorted to the altar to be sacrificed to Oniguma. Along the way, she tends to Fushi, who, despite having the body of a young man, doesn’t seem to have the mind of one.

title: A Large Vessel
overview: Hayase, interested in Fushi’s inability to die, brings him along with March, Parona and Pioran to the nation of Yanome. They are shown around town and treated well, but things are not as they seem.

title: Those Who Follow
overview: Fushi, March and Parona continue their escape from Yanome and back to Ninanna. Hayase chases after them, especially in order to capture Fushi.

title: Our Goals
overview: Having gained a semblance of humanity, Fushi sets off on his own. He encounters Pioran again, who teachers him words and writing, helping him live as a human being.

title: The Boy Who Wants to Change
overview: Fushi and Pioran arrive at Takunaha, where they meet Booze Man and a boy named Gugu. Gugu, who had been living with Booze Man for several months following an accident, warms up to Fushi.

title: Monster Brothers
overview: Fushi explains to Gugu how his form as the wolf, Joaan, and March, came to be. Gugu explains death to Fushi. Together, they come to understand the meaning of family.

title: Deep Memories
overview: Fushi tells Gugu about the wolf, Joaan, and the boy he took on the form of. Gugu explains death to Fushi, recalling his own past, working for a wealthy family with his brother.

title: New Family
overview: Fushi and Gugu fight against the Nokkers that they encountered in the forest. Though the situation is grim, Gugu has a trick up his sleeve.

title: Gift From the Past
overview: Four years after their encounter with the Nokkers, Fushi and Gugu have grown into fine young men. They’re invited to Rean’s birthday party after a brief reunion with Gugu’s brother.

title: Awakening
overview: Just as Gugu and Rean come to understand each other, the Nokkers emerge, causing panic in the party hall. Fushi and Gugu are forced to fight.

title: Aspiring to Go Higher
overview: Fushi leaves Booze Man’s home and sets out to wander aimlessly around Takunaha. He has many questions for the Observer, but can’t seem to get any straight answers.

title: Jananda, Island of Freedom
overview: Fushi and Pioran are separated on the prison island of Jananda. He enters a tournament where, if he survives until the end, he will be granted the right to do anything he wants – including leaving the island with Pioran.

title: A Girl Named Tonari
overview: Fushi transforms into Parona of Ninannah in the middle of the tournament. He is shocked when he realizes what this implies. Meanwhile, Tonari, desperate to have Fushi join her and her friends, offers to help rescue Pioran.

title: The Children's Dreams
overview: Fushi just barely manages to take down the Nokkers. He opens up to the others, and they discuss their dreams and pasts.

title: The Defeated
overview: Having defeated Fushi, Hayase makes a surprising proposal to the people of the island of Jananda.

title: To Continue On
overview: Tonari returns to Jananda to rescue Fushi. Before setting out again, they stay the night in a cave, and Tonari shares her past.

title: Wandering Rage
overview: Fushi, Tonari and their friends fight the Nokkers, who have taken over corpses.

title: Echoes
overview: Fushi parts ways with Tonari and heads to the port where Pioran is waiting for him. He worries that he may attract the Nokkers again and endanger her.

title: Infatuation Reborn
overview: Fushi, having spent several decades alone on an island, learns that a village was attacked by the Nokkers.

title: Beating Will
overview: Things do not go well when Tonari tries to remove the Nokker from Hisame's arm.

title: The Awaited
overview: Kahaku, the sixth of Hayase's descendants, introduces himself to Fushi. Much time has passed, and the Guardians have expanded their influence.

title: The Young Man Who Can See
overview: Fushi must stay on the move in order to keep the Nokkers from attacking, so Prince Bon accompanies him out of the castle town.

title: The Holy Man's Voyage
overview: Fushi, along with Kahaku, Bon and Bon's army, travel through several towns, spreading word of the Nokkers along the way.

title: Heretics Betrayed
overview: Kahaku asks Fushi to marry him. He seems confident that they can overcome the complications of Fushi being a shape-shifting immortal, but Fushi isn’t. Cylira returns.

title: Crime and Forgiveness
overview: Fushi attempts to escape from his iron cell, while Bon and Todo await their trial in their own cells.

title: Beyond Dreams
overview: The Kingdom of Ularis, along with Fushi, Todo, Bon's family, and Baron Spring Roll, prepare for life without Bon.

title: Expanding Consciousness
overview: As warned by Kahaku’s arm, the Nokkers attack the head church of the Church of Bennett, causing terrible devastation.

title: Resonance
overview: Fushi trains his senses in preparation for the Nokkers’ attack on Renril. He discovers a strange girl in a circus tent.

title: The Value of Flesh
overview: Having finished training on the derelict ship, Fushi travels to Renril, where the Nokkers are threatening to attack.

title: Secret Behind the Veil
overview: Fushi and his allies work to win over the trust of the people of Renril in preparation for the Nokkers’ attack.

title: The Wise Man's Identity
overview: The team works to prevent the Nokkers from making use of the waterways and sewers. Fushi works non-stop rebuilding all the homes, and the exhaustion begins to show.

title: Morning of Rebirth
overview: The city of Renril bands together around Fushi in preparation for the Nokkers’ attack. The princess introduces him to her father, King Boldron of Sonia.

title: The Self, Worn Down
overview: Fushi fends off the Nokkers miles away from Renril, but the Nokkers had already devised a more cunning plan.

title: Three Eternal Warriors
overview: The death of his three friends turns Fushi mad, and he begins defeating the Nokkers at a furious pace. But as it turns out, he may not be the only immortal.

title: What You Want to Protect
overview: Amidst the brutal battle against the Nokkers, Bon and Kahaku both share a desire to protect Fushi’s humanity. How they go about it, however, puts them at odds.

title: Death of the Deathless
overview: March begins her desperate search for Fushi, whose absence has led to the city being overrun by the Nokkers.

title: And Then, Dawn
overview: In the beginning, it was an orb. And Fushi was an orb once again. But though there was no certainty, there was hope.

title: End of an Era
overview: With the Nokkers defeated, the people of Renril rejoice, and Fushi and his friends discuss their hopes for the future.

title: Peaceful World
overview: Fushi awakens in a world where peace reigns. What new and old encounters await him?

title: A Knot in the Soul
overview: Fushi begins his new life in the present day and begins to gather his former classmates at Yuki's house.

title: Mizuha
overview: Mizuha is fed up with her everyday life and decides to run away.

title: The One Who Vanished
overview: Fushi takes care of a troubled Mizuha.

title: Unheard Words
overview: Fushi and his revived friends joins Yuki and Mizuha at school.

title: Proof of Peace
overview: Fushi struggles to maintain the peace between Tonari and Mizuha.

title: The Attack
overview: The mystery of the Nokker inside Mimori is clarified a little, as it seeks Fushi at the school to attack him.

title: Trouble
overview: Hirotoshi reveals Mimori's deeply troubled past.

title: Rejected Life
overview: Fushi and Bon try to save Mimori at Hirotoshi's request, but a new Nokker appears.

title: Become as People
overview: Rikuya is found destroyed. The Nokkers confront Fushi.

title: Their Home
overview: Fushi has an opportunity to deal with the Nokker in Izumi's body.

title: Flying Bug
overview: Fushi transforms into small creatures to keep an eye on Mizuha and Funa. Meanwhile, Eko gives Yuki an idea.

title: Number One
overview: Fushi goes back to school, but as Fuuna. He learns about what people really think.

title: Where One Belongs
overview: Gugu confesses to being homesick. Fushi struggles to come to terms with his friends growing distant.

title: Leaving Alone
overview: Bon describes how Fushi's friends spent their time after he went to sleep centuries prior.

title: Connective
overview: Fushi and his friends look for ways to get back in touch with Mizuha.

title: Invisible Battle
overview: Fushi tries desperately to negotiate with Mizuha. Meanwhile, an impostor attacks the school.

title: A Willingness to Believe
overview: Tonari, Aiko and their schoolmates fight the Fushi impostor.

title: Clean Water
overview: Yuki and the crew negotiate a peace with the Guardians.

title: Coexist
overview: Fushi and his friends return to Yuki's place to discuss how they should avoid being arrested.

title: Stay Here
overview: The class needs to decide what they're doing after middle school. Fushi's decision is much weightier.

title: To Fushi
overview: The Beholder leaves Fushi with a memo.
